question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: findByBlockType()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call findByBlockType(BlockType.LATEST) to exploit the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST cache TTL, causing the cached 'latest' block to become stale and appear frozen to all consumers for the duration of the cache window, effectively delaying perceived block progression by 500%+ of average block time?
